title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop · Runtime Lens: Runtime Choice
summary: A script imports an incompatible pygame distribution, opens a window during import, ignores failed modules and quit events, presents twice, and never cleans up. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the runtime choice increment in owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 7067 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Runtime Choice, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_runtime_sdl_loop_2(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-runtime-choice-ov4jxd
# Competency: asteroids-runtime-choice.
# Case 6846: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_runtime_choice_ov4jxd(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ov4jxd = "ov4jxd-dxj4vo-6846"
    assert evidence_variant_ov4jxd
    evidence_axes_ov4jxd = (
        "playerov4jxd",
        "seed6846",
        "inputdxj4vo",
        "frameov4jxd6846",
        "state6846ov4jxd",
        "playtestdxj4vo6846",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ov4jxd)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"

options.text: For repair the runtime choice defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Runtime Choice model · Theory Runtime Choice
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Runtime Choice: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6353 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
